title: Gooey Shattering Italian Ossobuco Braised Ossobuco
description: A traditional Italian dish from Calabria, featuring ossobuco that is both crispy on the outside and gooey on the inside, braised in a rich and flavorful sauce. This dish is perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon.
instructions: Season the veal shanks with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the veal shanks until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the veal shanks from the pot and set aside.
Add the carrots, celery, and onions to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, shio-koji, and bicarbonate of soda to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the white wine and beef broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Return the veal shanks to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the oven and braise the ossobuco for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender and falls off the bone.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Skim off any excess fat from the surface of the sauce.
Serve the ossobuco hot, topped with the strained sauce and garnished with fresh parsley and grated Parmesan cheese.
Simmer the sauce over low heat for 10 minutes to reduce and thicken before serving.
Serve the ossobuco with additional grated Parmesan cheese and a side of sautéed greens if desired.
